title: Sphere Object
description: Transient sphere. A transient sphere is not displayed or saved in a document. Transient spheres are used as a wrapper to work with raw sphere information. A transient sphere is a full sphere defined by a point and a radius. They are created statically using the create method of the Sphere class.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Sphere object.

methods.name: getData
methods.description: Gets all of the data defining the sphere.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Sets all of the data defining the sphere.

methods.name: transformBy
methods.description: Updates this surface by transforming it with a given input matrix.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: origin
properties.description: Gets and sets the origin point (center) of the sphere.

properties.name: radius
properties.description: Gets and sets the radius of the sphere.

classType.title: Sphere.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: Sphere.copy Method
copy.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Sphere object.
copy.return_description: Returns a new Sphere object that is a copy of this Sphere object.

create.title: Sphere.create Method
create.return_description: Returns the new Sphere object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The origin point (center) of the sphere.

getData.title: Sphere.getData Method
getData.description: Gets all of the data defining the sphere.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The output origin point (center) of the sphere.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: The output radius of the sphere.

set.title: Sphere.set Method
set.description: Sets all of the data defining the sphere.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The origin point (center) of the sphere.

transformBy.title: Sphere.transformBy Method
transformBy.description: Updates this surface by transforming it with a given input matrix.
transformBy.return_description: Returns true if the transform was successful.

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.description: A 3D matrix that defines the transform to apply to the surface.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sphere_var.evaluator
